executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict continues at MAJOR intensity (759 incidents/24h) with day 4 seeing direct US-Israeli strikes on Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, Qatar shooting down two Iranian bombers, and Kuwait recording its first military fatality. The IRGC has launched at least 12 waves of attacks targeting 500+ US and allied sites. Russia warns WWIII could start 'at any moment' while China mediates diplomatically. Gulf states remain under sustained missile/drone attack with UAE intercepting 163 targets in a single wave.
whatsqlite: US confirms 1,250+ targets struck inside Iran; Israel/USA attacked Natanz nuclear facility
Qatar shot down two Iranian bombers attempting to attack Gulf neighbors - first Iranian air attack on a Gulf state
UAE air defenses intercepted 9 ballistic missiles, 6 cruise missiles, and 148 drones in single attack wave
Kuwait announces first military death (Sergeant Walid Majeed Suleiman) in Iran-US conflict
US deployed new PrSM (Precision Strike Missile) against Iran for first time - significant escalation
US claims Iran has no ships left in Gulf of Oman - major naval defeat
Russia's Medvedev warns WWIII could start 'at any moment' if Trump continues current policy
Russian Pobeda airline to begin evacuation flights from Dubai/Abu Dhabi March 3
23 protesters killed in Pakistan over Khamenei's death - unrest continues
Israel struck Natanz nuclear facility according to Iranian statements
confirmed_events: Gulf oil/gas production remains shut - confirmed by multiple sources
Bahrain interceptors have brought down 70 missiles and 59 drones total (continues)
IRGC 12th wave attacked US targets in Kuwait, UAE, Bahrain, Strait of Hormuz with 26 drones and 5 missiles
US B-1 bombers conducting deep strikes inside Iranian territory
Hezbollah actively engaged against Israel - 52 Lebanese killed in Israeli strikes
unverified_emerging: IRGC claim that 'Athena Nova' fuel tanker burning in Strait of Hormuz - single source (Al Alam)
Iranian total naval/air blockade on Gulf countries - unverified social media claim
IRGC claim US tanker on fire in Strait of Hormuz - unverified as of last 4h
Pakistani speculation of being 'next target' dismissed by senior security official

people_travel: Russian Pobeda airline to begin evacuation flights from Dubai and Abu Dhabi March 3
Dubai airspace closed due to Iranian ballistic missile strikes - tourists stranded
supply_chain: Gulf oil and gas production remains shut down - ongoing
Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic disrupted by IRGC attacks
market_macro: Major escalation could further destabilize global oil markets beyond current production shutdown

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict enters critical phase with Iran suffering significant military defeats (navy destroyed, nuclear facilities struck, 1,250+ US targets hit inside Iran). IRGC continues 12+ waves of retaliation against Gulf allies. Israel-Hezbollah northern front active. Qatar and Bahrain actively defending against Iranian attacks. Risk of further Iranian desperation attacks on civilian infrastructure remains HIGH. US public opinion turning against war (43% disapprove).
escalation_risks: Iranian attacks on Gulf civilian infrastructure intensifying - hotels, schools struck
Russian warning of WWIII signals potential for great power escalation
Hezbollah/IRGC proxy attacks expanding to additional fronts
Pakistani instability spreading - 23 dead, could draw in additional actors
Qatar now directly engaging Iranian aircraft - risk of further Gulf state escalation
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: UAE and Qatar pressing Trump to end bombing campaign. Chinese Foreign Minister in contact with Iran offering diplomatic role. Iraq Foreign Minister warns of regional chaos. However, IRGC rhetoric remains defiant ('no safety for you anywhere'), and Trump administration framing this as 'regime-change war' suggests limited de-escalation appetite in short term. Key variables: Iranian naval/ballistic missile capability degradation rate and Gulf state willingness to continue absorbing attacks.
